sampleBriefing.label: Sample Issue
sampleBriefing.headline: This week in the Valley
sampleBriefing.intro: Every Monday, we score 30+ RGV business stories by the <nri>Nolana Relevance Index</nri>. Here are sample top stories — Pro subscribers get the full briefing.
sampleBriefing.disclaimer: Sample format shown for demonstration. Real issues include direct source links to public records, city agendas, filings, and local media.

about.label: About
about.headline: Published by the people who know your numbers.
about.body: Published by <link>{publisher}</link>, a McAllen-based bookkeeping and tax coordination firm that works closely with Valley business owners. We built The Nolana Report to turn public business signals into a weekly briefing owners can actually use.
about.methodBody: Every week, we review public sources including city agendas, business filings, economic development updates, bridge wait data, FX movement, local media, and regional announcements. Stories are scored by local business relevance, urgency, and potential market impact using the Nolana Relevance Index.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.headline: Common questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about The Nolana Report.

items.question: What is The Nolana Report?
items.answer: The Nolana Report is a weekly business intelligence briefing for the Rio Grande Valley, covering new businesses, government moves, cross-border trade, and industrial investment across McAllen, Edinburg, Brownsville, and the RGV. Published every Monday by National Bookkeeping Company.

items.question: What business news sources cover the Rio Grande Valley?
items.answer: The Nolana Report aggregates and scores 25+ RGV news sources including KRGV, ValleyCentral, RGV Business Journal, The Monitor, Brownsville Herald, Rio Grande Guardian, Texas Border Business, and Valley Business Report, plus government data from the Federal Register, CBP bridge wait times, and USD/MXN exchange rates.

items.question: How much does The Nolana Report cost?
items.answer: The Nolana Report offers three tiers: Free (weekly summary email + live data bar), Pro at $9/month (full 30-story briefing with score badges and archive access), and Intel at $19/month (Pro features plus monthly deep-dive industry analysis). Founding members get Pro locked at $7/month forever.

items.question: What new businesses are opening in McAllen and the RGV?
items.answer: The Nolana Report tracks new business openings, LLC filings, permits, and expansions across McAllen, Edinburg, Pharr, Mission, Brownsville, Harlingen, and the entire Rio Grande Valley every week. Subscribe free at nolanareport.com to get the latest.

items.question: What companies are investing in the Rio Grande Valley?
items.answer: The Nolana Report's Industrial & Investment Watch tracks manufacturing plants, corporate relocations, Foreign Trade Zone activity, and economic development announcements across the RGV, including EDC news from Edinburg, McAllen, Weslaco, and the Port of Brownsville.
